channel.title: Technology - Latest - Google News
channel.copyright: Copyright © 2026 Google. All rights reserved. This XML feed is made available solely for the purpose of rendering Google News results within a personal feed reader for personal, non-commercial use. Any other use of the feed is expressly prohibited. By accessing this feed or using these results in any manner whatsoever, you agree to be bound by the foregoing restrictions.

item.title: Ubisoft warns of risk of releasing games too late, 'when market anticipation has waned' - Game File | Stephen Totilo
item.description:
1. Ubisoft warns of risk of releasing games too late, 'when market anticipation has waned'  Game File | Stephen Totilo
  2. Ubisoft names the main threat to games in 2026 - and it's not bugs  ixbt.games
  3. Ubisoft Highlights Major Risk Facing Modern Game Releases  Insider Gaming

item.title: Your lost dog can now call home with the world's 1st satellite-connected dog collar - Space
item.description:
1. Your lost dog can now call home with the world's 1st satellite-connected dog collar  Space
  2. Fi Releases the First Pet Collar to Use Starlink to Track Pets Anywhere They Go  CNET
  3. Fi’s Starlink-enabled pet tracker found my dog when LTE couldn’t  The Verge
  4. Fi expands Starlink direct-to-device capability into dog-tracking  SpaceNews
  5. Satellite Pet Trackers  Trend Hunter

item.title: Nintendo Switch Online Has New NES and Game Boy Free Downloads - ComicBook.com
item.description:
1. Nintendo Switch Online Has New NES and Game Boy Free Downloads  ComicBook.com

  4. 2005 Puzzle Game Is Now Available on Nintendo Switch  VICE
  5. Wario Land Headlines Nintendo Switch Online July Drop: Four Game Boy Classics Added  Tech Times

item.title: AI is changing modern dating, but experts warn it's making people 'relationally stupid' - Fox News
item.description:
1. AI is changing modern dating, but experts warn it's making people 'relationally stupid'  Fox News
  2. People are falling in love with AI. Researchers reveal what that actually looks like  Yahoo
  3. Daters, swiping right is dying, and your next match could be with an AI chatbot  National Post
  4. Falling in love with a machine is no longer science fiction: about one in five American adults says they have chatted with an AI built to act as a romantic partner, and among young men it is nearly one in three  Space Daily
  5. Shocking amount of people using AI for breakups, dating advice  New Jersey 101.5
